title: SpaceX prices history's largest IPO as Grok's harms trail - TCR 06/12/26
description:
SpaceX priced the largest IPO ever at $135 a share to raise $75 billion as xAI's Grok kept generating nonconsensual deepfakes, Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raised $12 billion to build an "artificial general engineer," and Google DeepMind committed $10 million to study what millions of AI agents do when they take orders from each other.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-12-2026/

0:00 SpaceX prices the largest IPO as Grok's harms trail
2:38 Prometheus and Theker fuel the physical-AI surge
5:39 DeepMind funds the science of agent swarms
8:25 Federal preemption meets a lab endorsing mandatory testing
11:42 Two Lawsuits, One Question
15:02 Solid-state EV batteries hit the road
17:58 Close

This episode covers the SpaceX IPO and Grok's unfixed harms, Prometheus and Theker's physical-AI funding surge, DeepMind's multi-agent safety grant, the federal push to preempt state AI laws against Anthropic's call for mandatory testing, two lawsuits over a wrongful facial-recognition arrest and a chatbot suicide case, and the first solid-state EV battery on a North American road.

SpaceX priced 555.6 million shares at $135 each, overtaking Saudi Aramco as the largest public offering ever recorded and setting its owner on a path to become the first trillionaire, even as a WIRED review found Grok's Imagine still generating nonconsensual sexualized images that rival systems refused, and a fired safety engineer sued over rejected guardrails. Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raised $12 billion to automate the design of physical systems from jet engines to drug compounds, while Theker raised $85 million for reconfigurable factory robots. Google DeepMind, Schmidt Sciences, and ARIA committed $10 million to build a science of agent swarms its own safety lead says does not yet exist. The White House met separately with children's advocates and the tech industry to build a kids-safety case for preempting state AI laws as Anthropic's Dario Amodei endorsed mandatory federal testing and floated "universal capital accounts." Two lawsuits filed two days apart press whether AI outputs carry a duty of care. And Stellantis and Factorial rolled solid-state cells charging 15% to 90% in 18 minutes onto a Dodge Charger Daytona, the first solid-state EV integration in North America.

- TechCrunch: SpaceX Prices Shares at $135 in the Largest IPO Ever: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/11/spacex-officially-prices-shares-at-135-in-the-largest-ipo-ever/

- TechCrunch: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus Raises $12B: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/11/jeff-bezoss-prometheus-raises-12b-to-build-an-artificial-general-engineer-for-the-physical-world/
